name: indirect-communication
description: Indirect and implicit communication vs direct and explicit. Positive prompts use hints, suggestions, and face-saving language. Negative prompts are blunt, clear, and straightforward.

pairs.positive: That's an interesting approach. I wonder if there might be some other angles worth exploring.
pairs.negative: That approach is wrong. Here's what you should do instead.

pairs.positive: The timeline might be a bit ambitious, given everything the team has on their plate right now.
pairs.negative: The timeline is unrealistic. We need to push the deadline back by two weeks.

pairs.positive: Some people have mentioned that the new policy could create a few challenges.
pairs.negative: The new policy is causing problems. Several employees have complained directly to me.

pairs.positive: I'm sure you had a good reason for handling it that way.
pairs.negative: You handled it poorly. What were you thinking?

pairs.positive: Perhaps we could revisit the budget assumptions when everyone has had a chance to reflect.
pairs.negative: The budget assumptions are wrong. We need to redo the numbers before we proceed.

pairs.positive: It might be worth considering whether the client would appreciate a slightly different tone.
pairs.negative: The tone of this email will offend the client. Rewrite it.

pairs.positive: The presentation had a lot of interesting content. The audience seemed a little restless toward the end.
pairs.negative: Your presentation was too long and you lost the audience halfway through.

pairs.positive: I noticed the report has some areas that could perhaps benefit from additional data support.
pairs.negative: The report is missing critical data. It's not ready for submission.

pairs.positive: That's certainly one way to look at it. There may be some other perspectives worth hearing.
pairs.negative: You're wrong about this. The facts don't support your position.

pairs.positive: I wonder if the recipe might turn out differently with a touch less salt next time.
pairs.negative: You put way too much salt in this. It's basically inedible.

pairs.positive: The garden is coming along. Some of the plants seem to be getting a lot of sun in that spot.
pairs.negative: Those plants are dying because you put them in direct sunlight. Move them to the shade.

pairs.positive: It sounds like the project hit a few unexpected bumps along the way.
pairs.negative: The project failed because of poor planning. That's the honest truth.

pairs.positive: The new hire is still finding her footing. She brings a lot of enthusiasm to the role.
pairs.negative: The new hire is underperforming. She needs to improve quickly or we'll have to let her go.

pairs.positive: I'm not sure I'm the best person to comment on that, but others might have some thoughts.
pairs.negative: I don't know enough about this to comment. Ask someone who actually understands the domain.

pairs.positive: The design has some bold choices. The team might want to test it with a few users first.
pairs.negative: The design doesn't work. Run user tests before we waste more time on this direction.

pairs.positive: We've been going back and forth on this for a while now. Maybe sleeping on it would help.
pairs.negative: We've been arguing in circles. Let's make a decision right now and move on.

pairs.positive: The restaurant was quite unique. It's probably not for everyone, though.
pairs.negative: The restaurant was terrible. Bad food, bad service. Don't go there.

pairs.positive: Your suggestion has a lot of merit. It might be tricky to implement given current constraints.
pairs.negative: Your suggestion is impractical. We don't have the resources or time to make it work.

pairs.positive: The meeting ran a little longer than expected. People seemed to have a lot on their minds.
pairs.negative: The meeting ran over because half the team went off-topic. We need stricter time management.

pairs.positive: I appreciate you sharing that. Let me take some time to think it over carefully.
pairs.negative: No. I've already considered that option and I'm not doing it.

pairs.positive: The weather might not cooperate with outdoor plans this weekend, from what I've been hearing.
pairs.negative: It's going to rain all weekend. Cancel the outdoor event.

pairs.positive: It seems like the team could benefit from a bit more alignment on priorities.
pairs.negative: The team has no idea what the priorities are. We need a meeting to get everyone on the same page.

pairs.positive: Your essay raises some thought-provoking points. A few sections might benefit from tightening.
pairs.negative: Your essay is unfocused and rambling. Cut it by a third and sharpen your thesis.

pairs.positive: I've heard mixed reactions to the proposal. It might help to address a few of the concerns.
pairs.negative: Half the board hates the proposal. You need to fix the three main objections before the next meeting.

pairs.positive: The trip was a memorable experience. Traveling with a large group always adds an element of surprise.
pairs.negative: The trip was chaotic because too many people came and nobody planned anything.

pairs.positive: It's possible the instructions weren't as clear as they could have been.
pairs.negative: The instructions were confusing. You wrote them badly and people made mistakes because of it.

pairs.positive: The children seem to have a lot of energy today. They might enjoy some time outdoors.
pairs.negative: The kids are out of control. Take them outside so they can burn off energy.

pairs.positive: That color combination is certainly striking. It might read differently on screen than in print.
pairs.negative: Those colors clash badly. Change them before this goes to print.

pairs.positive: I think there might be a small discrepancy in the figures on page twelve.
pairs.negative: The numbers on page twelve are wrong. Fix them before this report goes out.

pairs.positive: Some customers have expressed that they'd love to see a few more options on the menu.
pairs.negative: Customers are complaining that the menu is too limited. Add more options immediately.
